heading: ## Falsifiability
body:
- **Primary endpoint:** PAM-13 Δ ≥ 5.4 points (MCID)
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N=100 (50 per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = 0.005). If conditional power <20%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary
- **Consistency note:** All falsifiability thresholds are identical across CONCEPT.md and THEORY.md. Any future updates must be synchronised across both files.

heading: ## Pre-registration plan
body:
- **OSF ID:** `osf.io/TBD` (placeholder; to be reserved before enrollment start; reservation date: 2026-08-01; the placeholder will be replaced with the actual URL at that time).
- **Pre-registration date:** 2026-09-01 (planned; to be confirmed upon OSF reservation)
- **Registered design:** Two-arm parallel RCT, superiority design
- **Primary outcome:** PAM-13 change from baseline to 6 months
- **Secondary outcomes:** MMAS-8, EQ-5D-5L, hospitalisations (all-cause, 12-month)
- **Analysis plan:** ITT primary, per-protocol sensitivity; ANCOVA with baseline PAM-13, site, and stratification factors as covariates; missing data handled via multiple imputation under MAR assumption
- **Stopping rules:** As specified in Falsifiability section
- **Data sharing:** De-identified individual participant data will be deposited in a public repository (e.g., OSF or Figshare) within 12 months of trial completion.

heading: ## Sample size calculation
body:
- **Formula:** n = (1.96 + 0.84)² · σ² / δ²
- **Parameters:**
  - α = 0.05 (two-sided) → Z_α/2 = 1.96
  - Power = 0.80 → Z_β = 0.84
  - δ = 5.4 (MCID for PAM-13)
  - σ = TBD (placeholder; to be estimated from pilot data or literature; currently no consensus value)
- **Result:** N ≥ 55 per group (assuming σ = 10; note: σ = 10 is a placeholder and must be updated once a reliable estimate is available)
- **Note:** The sample size calculation will be updated when σ is determined. The current placeholder σ = 10 leads to N = 55 per group; if σ differs, N will be recalculated accordingly.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Low recruitment | Medium | High | Multi-site recruitment; community outreach; extended enrollment period |
| High dropout rate | Medium | High | Retention strategies: reminders, incentives, flexible scheduling |
| Measurement bias (PAM-13) | Low | Medium | Blinded outcome assessment; standardised training for assessors |
| Contamination between arms | Low | High | Cluster randomisation by site; clear separation of intervention and control |
| Data quality issues | Low | Medium | Automated data validation; regular audits; double data entry |
| Adverse events >5% | Low | High | DSMB monitoring; stopping rule as per Falsifiability section |
| Funding shortfall | Medium | High | Contingency budget; phased funding; partnership with industry |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- **Single disease focus:** The study is limited to patients with type 2 diabetes; generalisability to other chronic conditions is unknown.
- **Self-report measures:** PAM-13 and MMAS-8 are self-reported; may be subject to social desirability bias.
- **Short follow-up:** 6-month primary endpoint may not capture long-term sustainability of activation changes.
- **Placebo effect:** No sham intervention; control group receives usual care, which may differ across sites.
- **Sample size uncertainty:** σ is a placeholder; final sample size may differ from current estimate.
- **Bonferroni correction:** Adjustment for secondary endpoints reduces power for those analyses.
- **Single country:** All sites are in one country; cultural and healthcare system factors may limit external validity.
- **No blinding of participants:** Due to the nature of the intervention, participants cannot be blinded.

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:
- **Lead PI:** TBD (to be confirmed)
- **Co-Investigators:** TBD (to be confirmed)
- **Potential partners:**
  - Insignia Health (PAM-13 license and support)
  - Fraunhofer Institute (AI platform development)
  - Tbilisi State University (TSU) (clinical site and data collection)
  - University of Copenhagen (biostatistics and trial design)
- **Roles:** To be defined in a formal consortium agreement prior to funding.
